Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.

end of world model.

If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.

- id=5: G, 9 pixels, rows 35-37, cols 38-40

Let me try to get all red blocks into this hole. Currently, the white structure faces DOWN with red blocks below. If I rotate to face UP, the red blocks would be above the white structure. If the green structure is above, then moving UP might push the red blocks into the green structure's hole.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Current state: facing DOWN after SPACE, then 2x RIGHT
# Need to get back to a state where I can align red blocks with green hole
# Let me try rotating to face UP (2 SPACEs from DOWN) and moving RIGHT

result = action(['SPACE', 'SPACE'])
print("After 2x SPACE (face UP):", result['state'], "done:", result.get('done'), "level_completed:", result.get('level_completed'))

seg = current_frame.segmentation
r_blocks = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'R']
g_blocks = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'G']
print(f"R blocks: {len(r_blocks)}, G blocks: {len(g_blocks)}")
for n in r_blocks + g_blocks:
    b = n['boundary']
    rows = [p[0] for p in b]
    cols = [p[1] for p in b]
    print(f"  id={n['id']} color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} rows=[{min(rows)},{max(rows)}] cols=[{min(cols)},{max(cols)}]")

# Try moving RIGHT to align with green structure
if not result.get('level_completed'):
    result2 = action(['RIGHT'])
    print("\nAfter RIGHT:", result2['state'], "done:", result2.get('done'), "level_completed:", result2.get('level_completed'))
    
    seg2 = current_frame.segmentation
    r_blocks2 = [n for n in seg2['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'R']
    g_blocks2 = [n for n in seg2['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'G']
    print(f"R blocks: {len(r_blocks2)}, G blocks: {len(g_blocks2)}")
    for n in r_blocks2 + g_blocks2:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  id={n['id']} color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} rows=[{min(rows)},{max(rows)}] cols=[{min(cols)},{max(cols)}]")
    
    if not result2.get('level_completed'):
        result3 = action(['RIGHT'])
        print("\nAfter 2nd RIGHT:", result3['state'], "done:", result3.get('done'), "level_completed:", result3.get('level_completed'))
        
        seg3 = current_frame.segmentation
        r_blocks3 = [n for n in seg3['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'R']
        g_blocks3 = [n for n in seg3['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'G']
        print(f"R blocks: {len(r_blocks3)}, G blocks: {len(g_blocks3)}")
        for n in r_blocks3 + g_blocks3:
            b = n['boundary']
            rows = [p[0] for p in b]
            cols = [p[1] for p in b]
            print(f"  id={n['id']} color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} rows=[{min(rows)},{max(rows)}] cols=[{min(cols)},{max(cols)}]")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
